semanticCore: Modality, state, manner, or asking how
quranicRelevance: Interrogative particle ('kayfa') asking about states, methods, or conditions of life and creation.

keyDerivations.meaning: How, in what manner, or how is it

figurativeOrRareUsages: كَيْفَ تَكْفُرُونَ بِاللَّهِ: How can you disbelieve in God? (rhetorical question expressing wonder/condemnation).
discardedNoise: Grammatical classification of kayfa as a noun of state.
summaryEn: The root serves as the primary interrogative particle for quality and state, used in the Quran to prompt reflection on creation and divine power.
summaryAr: يخدم هذا الجذر كأداة استفهام رئيسية عن الكيفية والحال، ويُستخدم في القرآن للفت الانتباه والتفكر في الخلق والقدرة الإلهية.
rawText: تَكَيَّفَ 5 : see what next precedes. كَيَّفْتُهُ فَتَكَيَّفَ 2 , used by the Muslim theologians, (K, TA,) verbs derived from كَيْفَ, (TA,) [signifying I specified by the ascription of its quality and it became so specified,] are formed in accordance with analogy, not heard from the Arabs. (K, * TA.) كَيْفُوفِيَّةٌ for كَيْفِيَّةٌ: see تَيْسِيَّةٌ. كَيْفِيَّةٌ Quality as answering to “ how? ”; mode, or manner, of being.
